title: lasthenia microglossa
intro: Lasthenia microglossa, also known as San Mateo woolly threads, is a rare, annual plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Lasthenia microglossa can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and processing the plant material to create capsules or tinctures.
